suggestion_reasons.Decision Rationale: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 here but the user still ranked the original preference draft first — the user explicitly called out missing rejected alternatives in the original preference draft as their 'main gripe', confirming this criterion captures the right thing. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Preventive Information Placement: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and the user praised the pre-emptive service registry warning as 'exactly where warnings belong', confirming this criterion is well-calibrated. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Narrative Header Flow: The rubric-guided draft scored only 2 here while the original preference draft scored 3, and the user ranked the original preference draft first specifically citing headers that 'tell a story' and the exp-field tip as front-loaded context — the current criterion captures the right goal but the description needs to make clear that sub-headers within a document must also follow the pattern, not just the top-level title. Strengthened the description and added a dimension explicitly requiring multi-level header coverage so the rubric-guided draft can't satisfy this criterion with a single top-level header.
suggestion_reasons.Empathy-Driven Troubleshooting: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 here and the user did not cite troubleshooting structure as a reason for preferring the original preference draft, so this criterion is working correctly. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Audience Calibration: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and the user's preference for the original preference draft was driven by structure and teaching style rather than calibration failures, so this criterion is functioning well. Kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Self-Service Completeness: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 here, but the user praised the original preference draft's 'minimal and runnable' code examples with 'clear context' — the current criterion rewards quantity of completeness artifacts but doesn't enforce that code snippets must be minimal, standalone, and immediately runnable without surrounding scaffolding. Added a dimension requiring that snippets be self-contained and executable in isolation to capture this preference.
suggestion_reasons.Proactive Contextual Teaching: Added a new criterion to capture the user's explicit praise for the 'differs from some systems you may have used' note and the exp-field tip as teaching moves that help engineers build correct mental models without waiting for failure — this is distinct from Decision Rationale (which covers architectural why-not) and no existing criterion covers inline teaching moments that orient the reader relative to prior experience or that preempt misapplied assumptions from other systems.

suggestion_reasons.Decision Rationale: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and ranked first on this criterion, and the user's comments specifically praised the pre-section warnings and rejected-alternative context as 'exactly the kind of pitfall context that actually teaches someone new to the system' — keeping this criterion exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Preventive Information Placement: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and ranked first, and the user explicitly called out warning placement before implementation as a key differentiator — keeping this criterion exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Narrative Header Flow: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and ranked first, and the user praised the walkthrough-style header 'What Happens When Your Token Gets Close to Expiring' by name while criticizing the original preference draft's bare-noun title — keeping this criterion exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Precise Technical Tone: The rubric-guided draft scored 5, but the user's feedback on the original preference draft ('restates the code rather than teaching it' and 'why 60 seconds and not 30 or 120?') reveals a gap this criterion doesn't fully cover: code prose should explain the reasoning behind specific values and choices, not just describe what the code does mechanically. Added a new dimension to capture this.
suggestion_reasons.Audience Calibration: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and ranked first on this criterion, and the user's feedback does not reveal any gap here — keeping this criterion exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Self-Service Completeness: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and ranked first on this criterion, and the user's feedback does not reveal any gap here — keeping this criterion exactly as-is.
